title: Living Arrangements of Adults by Age Group and Sex (row for 2023)
survey: Current Population Survey, Annual Social and Economic Supplement (CPS ASEC)
units: percent of the age x sex population (columns), with the total population in thousands

provenance.source: U.S. Census Bureau, Historical Living Arrangements of Adults, Table AD-3 (Living Arrangements of Adults by Age Group), five age-band files
provenance.fetch_method: direct HTTPS GET of the published .xls time-series files from www2.census.gov (keyless, authoritative); parsed with xlrd; the 2023 row of each age-band file transcribed verbatim.
provenance.category_note: AD-3 categories are mutually exclusive shares of the age x sex population: living_alone, living_with_spouse (married, spouse present), child_of_householder (living in a parent's home as the householder's child; absent from the 65-74 and 75+ tables), living_with_partner (unmarried cohabiting partner), living_with_other_relatives, living_with_nonrelatives. The PSID coresident_spouse concept covers MX8 codes 20 AND 22, i.e. married spouse OR partner, so the concept-matched Census anchor for coresident_spouse is living_with_spouse + living_with_partner (the decomposition names this).

band_mapping_to_psid.note: CPS AD-3 bands do not match the PSID gate bands one-for-one. The anchor decomposition maps: PSID 15-24 -> CPS 18-24 (PSID includes ages 15-17, near-universally with a parent and unpartnered; named age-floor delta); PSID 25-34 -> CPS 25-34 (clean); PSID 35-44, 45-54, 55-64 -> the single CPS 35-64 band (named band-aggregation delta); PSID 65-74 -> CPS 65-74 (clean); PSID 75+ -> CPS 75+ (clean).
